SHOULD:processConsumer

SHALL:able-to-populateProducer

SHALL:able-to-populateProducer

SHALL:able-to-populateProducer

SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:processConsumer
comparisonStudy O0..*EHDSImagingReportDocumentation (reference) of a prior Imaging Report to which the current images were compared.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:processConsumer
dicomStudyMetadata O0..*EHDSImagingStudyMetadata of the DICOM study. A study comprises a set of series, each of which includes a set of Service-Object Pair Instances (SOP Instances - images or other data) acquired or produced in a common context. A series is of only one modality (e.g. X-ray, CT, MR, ultrasound), but a study may have multiple series of different modalities.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:processConsumer
attachments O0..*EHDSAttachmentReport attachments
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:processConsumer

Documentation for this format

name: EHDSImagingReportObligations
title: Imaging Report Obligations
description: Obligations for the logical model of imaging report.

element.short: Imaging report model
element.definition: Imaging report reflects the observations and interpretations of one or more imaging studies, contains elements such as the reason why the study is requested, relevant contextual medical information, the modality used to acquire images and its settings, procedures and body localisations that were used, a description of the observations and findings, exposure information, conclusion and advice.

element.short: Imaging report header
element.definition: Basic metadata for this information

element.short: Patient/subject information
element.definition: Patient/subject information

element.short: Unique identifier of the document
element.definition: Business identifier for the object, unique within its system. Supporting disambiguation between different contexts (systems/countries).

element.short: The author of the report.
element.definition: The author of the report.

element.short: Date of finalising/issuing the document
element.definition: Date and optionally time of authoring/issuing

element.short: Status of the report
element.definition: Status of the report

element.short: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.
element.definition: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.

element.short: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.
element.definition: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

element.short: Type of document (e.g. 18748-4 Diagnostic imaging study)
element.definition: Type of document (e.g. 18748-4 Diagnostic imaging study)

element.short: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'
element.definition: Human readable document title that can be displayed in search results, etc. This can be documentType's display name, or it can be assembled from multiple elements. Examples: 'Laboratory Result Report', 'Patient Summary of Jane Green 10.12.2024'

element.short: Time of service that is being documented
element.definition: Time of service that is being documented

element.short: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian
element.definition: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian

element.definition: Document attestation details

element.definition: Attester who validated the document

element.definition: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester

element.definition: Document legal authentication details

element.definition: The person or organisation taking responsibility for the content of the document.

element.definition: Date and time when the document was authorised

element.short: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. imaging study types, body regions, modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.
element.definition: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. imaging study types, body regions, modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

element.short: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)
element.definition: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)

element.short: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.
element.definition: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.

element.short: Accession number - an identifier, managed by the RIS at the local level, which usually uniquely identifies an imaging procedure request, and links it to imaging study(ies) and related imaging report(s).
element.definition: Accession number - an identifier, managed by the RIS at the local level, which usually uniquely identifies an imaging procedure request, and links it to imaging study(ies) and related imaging report(s).

element.short: Information recipient (intended recipient or recipients of the report, additional recipients might be identified by the ordering party, e.g. GP, other specialist), if applicable
element.definition: Information recipient (intended recipient or recipients of the report, additional recipients might be identified by the ordering party, e.g. GP, other specialist), if applicable

element.short: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document
element.definition: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document

element.short: Imaging report structured body
element.definition: Imaging report structured body

element.short: Order Information (Imaging Result Report could respond to multiple orders).
element.definition: Order Information (Imaging Result Report could respond to multiple orders).

element.short: Identifier(s) of the imaging service order.
element.definition: Identifier(s) of the imaging service order.

element.short: Date and time of the order placement.
element.definition: Date and time of the order placement.

element.short: The person/organisation "authorised" to place the order. Order placer could be either a health professional, health professional organisation or the patient himself.
element.definition: The person/organisation "authorised" to place the order. Order placer could be either a health professional, health professional organisation or the patient himself.

element.short: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested.
element.definition: An explanation or justification for why this service is being requested.

element.short: Specification of clinical question (goal of the investigation) to be answered by the imaging investigation.
element.definition: Specification of clinical question (goal of the investigation) to be answered by the imaging investigation.

element.short: Additional clinical information about the patient or specimen that may affect service delivery or interpretation (additional types of information may be allowed in implementations).
element.definition: This information includes diagnosis, clinical findings and other observations. This includes observations explicitly requested by the producer (filler) to provide context or supporting information needed to complete the order. For example, reporting the metal implants present in patient's body.

element.short: Clinical findings and other observations (e.g., height and weights of the patient).
element.definition: Clinical findings and other observations (e.g., height and weights of the patient).

element.short: Conditions that may influence the service or result interpretation.
element.definition: Conditions that may influence the service or result interpretation.

element.short: Medication administered before ordering the service specifically for this procedure.
element.definition: Medication administered before ordering the service specifically for this procedure.

element.short: List of implants or devices that affect the course of the examination or its interpretation (e.g. metal implants).
element.definition: List of implants or devices that affect the course of the examination or its interpretation (e.g. metal implants).

element.short: Pregnancy status when the imaging examination was performed (e.g., pregnant, not pregnant, unknown).
element.definition: Pregnancy status when the imaging examination was performed (e.g., pregnant, not pregnant, unknown).

element.short: Sex parameter for clinical use - provides guidance on how a recipient should apply settings or reference ranges that are derived from observable information such as an organ inventory, recent hormone lab tests, genetic testing, menstrual status, obstetric history, etc. This property is intended for use in clinical decision making, and indicates that treatment or diagnostic tests should consider best practices associated with the relevant reference population.
element.definition: Sex parameter for clinical use - provides guidance on how a recipient should apply settings or reference ranges that are derived from observable information such as an organ inventory, recent hormone lab tests, genetic testing, menstrual status, obstetric history, etc. This property is intended for use in clinical decision making, and indicates that treatment or diagnostic tests should consider best practices associated with the relevant reference population.

element.short: Specimen information. Note: A specimen (not attached to a body) can be used for diagnostic, forensic and medical research purposes.
element.definition: Specimen information. Note: A specimen (not attached to a body) can be used for diagnostic, forensic and medical research purposes.

element.short: Information on total exposure to ionising radiation. This information is required by regulations in several EU countries.
element.definition: Information on total exposure to ionising radiation. This information is required by regulations in several EU countries.
element.requirements: eHN Guideline IMG (v1.1): B.1.4.4, Article 2 Imaging report definition

element.short: Examination report content.
element.definition: Examination report content.

element.short: Imaging modality (or modalities) used during imaging investigation (DICOM CID029).
element.definition: Imaging modality (or modalities) used during imaging investigation (DICOM CID029).

element.short: All body parts investigated
element.definition: All body parts investigated

element.short: Imaging procedure performed during imaging study.
element.definition: Imaging procedure performed during imaging study.

element.short: Information about medications administered (contrast, sedation, stress agents, etc) during the procedure.
element.definition: Information about medications administered (contrast, sedation, stress agents, etc) during the procedure.

element.short: Adverse reactions manifested during imaging investigation.
element.definition: Adverse reactions manifested during imaging investigation.

element.short: Imaging report result data (report could consist of multiple observations)
element.definition: Imaging report result data (report could consist of multiple observations)
element.requirements: eHN Guideline IMG (v1.1): A.5.4, A.5.4.1, A.5.4.3

element.short: A concise and clinically contextualised summary including interpretation/impression of the diagnostic report.
element.definition: A concise and clinically contextualised summary including interpretation/impression of the diagnostic report.

element.short: Narrative description of the clinical conclusion (impression).
element.definition: Narrative description of the clinical conclusion (impression).

element.short: Condition or finding from imaging investigation.
element.definition: Condition or finding from imaging investigation.

element.short: Recommendation section for additional imaging tests or other actions
element.definition: Recommendation section for additional imaging tests or other actions

element.short: Narrative description of the recommended activities including additional test, medication etc.
element.definition: Narrative description of the recommended activities including additional test, medication etc.

element.short: A complex and structured information about recommended goals, activities and objectives in the form of one or more formal care plan.
element.definition: A complex and structured information about recommended goals, activities and objectives in the form of one or more formal care plan.

element.short: Documentation (reference) of a prior Imaging Report to which the current images were compared.
element.definition: Documentation (reference) of a prior Imaging Report to which the current images were compared.

element.short: Metadata of the DICOM study. A study comprises a set of series, each of which includes a set of Service-Object Pair Instances (SOP Instances - images or other data) acquired or produced in a common context. A series is of only one modality (e.g. X-ray, CT, MR, ultrasound), but a study may have multiple series of different modalities.
element.definition: Metadata of the DICOM study. A study comprises a set of series, each of which includes a set of Service-Object Pair Instances (SOP Instances - images or other data) acquired or produced in a common context. A series is of only one modality (e.g. X-ray, CT, MR, ultrasound), but a study may have multiple series of different modalities.

element.short: Report attachments
element.definition: Report attachments
